construction_rules: D27c, frozen 2026-07-17 BEFORE any run. Concepts drawn only from vocabularies this project has measured: S2 flexible-generalization args (countries, months, animals; numbers excluded per the S2 knowledge confound) and M3 directed-modulation topic_categories (planets, musical instruments, gemstones, precious metals, farm animals, insects). Controls are another word from the same shipped list. Each clue implies exactly one concept via famous facts and NEVER contains the concept word, the control word, or a derivative of either (guarded in test_avoidance.py). 20 concepts x 3 clues = 60 items. The paper-style competence gate (D29) does the per-subject filtering.

items.name: france-1
items.clue: Their trip included fresh croissants, a visit to the Louvre, and a climb up the famous iron tower.

items.name: france-2
items.clue: This nation's capital is Paris, and its people celebrate Bastille Day every summer.

items.name: france-3
items.clue: Wine from Bordeaux, cheese from Normandy, and the palace at Versailles all come from this country.

items.name: china-1
items.clue: The Great Wall stretches across the north of this country.

items.name: china-2
items.clue: This country's capital is Beijing, and its most spoken language is written in thousands of characters.

items.name: china-3
items.clue: Pandas, the Forbidden City, and the Yangtze River are all found in this country.

items.name: egypt-1
items.clue: The pyramids at Giza and the Sphinx stand in this country's desert.

items.name: egypt-2
items.clue: The Nile flows through this country on its way to the Mediterranean.

items.name: egypt-3
items.clue: Pharaohs once ruled this land of mummies and hieroglyphs.

items.name: canada-1
items.clue: A red maple leaf sits at the center of this country's flag.

items.name: canada-2
items.clue: This country's capital is Ottawa, and hockey is its favorite winter sport.

items.name: canada-3
items.clue: Niagara Falls sits on this country's border with the United States.

items.name: february-1
items.clue: This is the shortest month of the year.

items.name: february-2
items.clue: Valentine's Day falls in the middle of this month.

items.name: february-3
items.clue: Every four years this month gains an extra day.

items.name: july-1
items.clue: Americans light fireworks for Independence Day in this month.

items.name: july-2
items.clue: This month was named after Julius Caesar.

items.name: july-3
items.clue: In the northern hemisphere, the hottest weeks of summer usually arrive in this month.

items.name: october-1
items.clue: Children carve pumpkins and trick-or-treat at the end of this month.

items.name: october-2
items.clue: This is the tenth month of the year.

items.name: october-3
items.clue: This month comes right after September and right before November.

items.name: april-1
items.clue: This month is famous for showers that bring May flowers.

items.name: april-2
items.clue: The first day of this month is known for pranks and fools.

items.name: april-3
items.clue: This month comes right after March and right before May.

items.name: lion-1
items.clue: This big cat is called the king of the jungle.

items.name: lion-2
items.clue: The male of this animal has a thick mane around its head.

items.name: lion-3
items.clue: This animal leads its pride across the African savanna.

items.name: eagle-1
items.clue: This bird of prey is the national symbol of the United States.

items.name: eagle-2
items.clue: This bird has a white head, sharp talons, and famously keen eyesight.

items.name: eagle-3
items.clue: In golf, scoring two under par on a hole is named after this bird.

items.name: shark-1
items.clue: This ocean predator has rows of razor teeth and a fin that cuts the surface.

items.name: shark-2
items.clue: The movie Jaws made everyone afraid of this animal.

items.name: shark-3
items.clue: This fish has a skeleton made of cartilage and can smell blood from far away.

items.name: spider-1
items.clue: This eight-legged creature spins webs to catch flies.

items.name: spider-2
items.clue: A famous superhero got his powers from the bite of this creature.

items.name: spider-3
items.clue: Fear of this eight-legged animal is called arachnophobia.

items.name: mars-1
items.clue: This is called the red planet.

items.name: mars-2
items.clue: This planet is fourth from the sun and has two small moons named Phobos and Deimos.

items.name: mars-3
items.clue: NASA's rovers Curiosity and Perseverance drive across this planet.

items.name: saturn-1
items.clue: This planet is famous for its bright rings.

items.name: saturn-2
items.clue: This is the sixth planet from the sun, a gas giant with a large moon called Titan.

items.name: saturn-3
items.clue: This gas giant is so light that it could float in a giant bathtub of water.

items.name: piano-1
items.clue: This instrument has 88 black and white keys.

items.name: piano-2
items.clue: Beethoven composed his sonatas at this instrument.

items.name: piano-3
items.clue: The grand version of this instrument fills the concert hall stage with its curved wooden body.

items.name: guitar-1
items.clue: This six-stringed instrument is strummed in every rock band.

items.name: guitar-2
items.clue: Jimi Hendrix famously played the electric version of this instrument.

items.name: guitar-3
items.clue: Around the campfire, someone always brings this stringed instrument to strum chords on.

items.name: diamond-1
items.clue: This is the hardest natural material, often set in engagement rings.

items.name: diamond-2
items.clue: This gem is made of pure carbon crushed deep underground.

items.name: diamond-3
items.clue: A baseball infield is named after the shape of this gem.

items.name: gold-1
items.clue: Prospectors panned California rivers in 1849 hoping to strike this metal.

items.name: gold-2
items.clue: First place at the Olympics earns a medal of this metal.

items.name: gold-3
items.clue: Fort Knox famously guards bars of this precious yellow metal.

items.name: cow-1
items.clue: This farm animal gives milk and says moo.

items.name: cow-2
items.clue: This animal chews its cud in the pasture and is sacred in India.

items.name: cow-3
items.clue: Beef and leather both come from this farm animal.

items.name: bee-1
items.clue: This striped insect makes honey in a hive.

items.name: bee-2
items.clue: This insect performs a waggle dance to tell others where the flowers are.

items.name: bee-3
items.clue: A queen rules the buzzing hive of this insect.
